what_it_does: This file centralizes the Vi 19-theme brand palette as JavaScript data and helpers: it exports the theme list, a V-symbol processing-state-to-theme map, accessor functions, a state-color resolver, and a generator that emits CSS custom-property declarations for every theme.
observations: Contains 19 named themes, each with a primary and accent hex value.
Classic-gold is treated as a shared identity anchor, using #E3C421 primary and #BD922B accent.
The remaining themes are muted, broad palette entries.
STATE_MAP maps V processing states (idle, launching, thinking, tool-use, error, success) to theme names.
Provides paletteCssVars() which returns a :host,:root CSS block with --palette-<name>-primary and --palette-<name>-accent variables.
Written as plain JS so it can load in both Vite and a raw-ESM verification harness.
Includes comments citing a SQL seed file and a 2026-06-16 design decision about the company gold.
standouts: Classic-gold is explicitly harmonized to the company gold while the rest of the muted palette is preserved.
The state map is framed as a mode-signal mechanism that re-tints the whole .vi-app via a cascaded anchor token.
The module claims to be the only place raw palette hex is allowed, with enforcement by a design-lint test.
conventions: Exports named constants and pure functions.
Uses Object.fromEntries to build a name-indexed lookup.
Uses Array.flatMap to generate paired CSS variable lines.
Names themes in kebab-case and maps semantic states to theme names.
Plain JS rather than JSON or TypeScript.
concerns: Raw hex values are hardcoded with no external source or runtime validation.
STATE_MAP is an exported mutable object, so consumers could alter global behavior.
paletteCssVars returns a string; it does not itself inject or scope the CSS.
No input validation in getTheme or resolveStateColor for unknown names/states.
Comments embed personal names and dates that may become stale.
notes: A centralized palette data module that feeds theme tokens, state-color resolution, and CSS variable generation for the Vi surface.
questions: How does the design-lint test actually enforce that no other file contains raw hex?
Which components consume these tokens and how is the state map wired to .vi-app re-tinting?
Is the order of themes significant, or is classic-gold's position intentional for fallback?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches what it does: it consolidates palette definitions and exposes helpers for lookup, state resolution, and CSS variable generation.
apparent_themes: Mode-driven color signaling via state mapping
Muted palette with a single high-saturation anchor
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to merge three legacy color definitions into one authoritative, easy-to-reference palette and to turn V-symbol processing states into a felt, app-wide identity shift through a single cascaded color token.
novelty: The file doubles as both a data registry and a CSS-variable generator, and it explicitly inverts the usual 'only JSON for tokens' pattern by using plain JS to stay loadable in multiple build/test environments.
contribution: Provides a stable, centralized palette data structure and a small helper API for theme lookup, state-to-color resolution, and CSS custom-property generation that a fused system can keep and reference.
summary_for_embedding: A plain-JS palette module for the Vi surface that defines 19 brand themes, maps V-symbol processing states to theme names, and generates corresponding CSS custom properties.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single authoritative source of Vi brand color tokens and state-driven theme resolution.

symbols.does: Returns the full array of theme objects.
symbols.description: A simple accessor that exposes the THEMES array, allowing consumers to enumerate every defined brand theme and its primary/accent values.

symbols.does: Looks up a single theme by its kebab-case name.
symbols.description: Uses the byName index to return the matching theme object or null, providing direct access to a specific palette entry without exposing the internal array.

symbols.does: Returns a shallow copy of the state-to-theme-name mapping.
symbols.description: Spreads STATE_MAP into a new object so callers can read which theme name corresponds to each V-symbol processing state without mutating the original map.

symbols.does: Resolves a V-symbol state to its theme colors.
symbols.description: Maps a state string through STATE_MAP, falls back to idle, and returns the theme name plus primary and accent hex values for that state.

symbols.does: Generates a CSS custom-property block for all themes.
symbols.description: Builds a :host,:root rule containing --palette-<name>-primary and --palette-<name>-accent declarations for every theme, suitable for injection into a stylesheet.
